evaluation.manager_summary: David called seeking alcohol treatment on his doctor's recommendation, reporting daily drinking for years. Agent completed basic insurance capture and minimal screening but missed critical rapport-building in the opening, did not assess withdrawal risk or urgency despite significant use history, and provided vague next steps. Caller raised concerns about family and cost that were addressed minimally without acknowledgment of the emotional weight.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: Agent jumped directly into clinical questions ('How much are you drinking?') within 10 seconds without any acknowledgement of the difficulty of the call or building rapport. No use of caller's name, no emotional acknowledgement, and a transactional tone throughout.
dimension_scores.coaching_note: Begin by acknowledging the courage it takes to make this call and thanking David for reaching out before moving to intake questions.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: Agent captured carrier (Summit Select) and member ID but did not obtain group number. When caller didn't have it, agent said they'd 'try to look it up' but provided no alternative framing. Basic verification completed but missing key field and no discussion of verification as helping rather than gatekeeping.
dimension_scores.coaching_note: Always capture or schedule follow-up for the group number, and frame verification as 'helping us get you the maximum benefits' rather than a checkbox.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: Agent asked about drinking amount and prior treatment but missed critical severity indicators like last use, withdrawal symptoms, or frequency beyond 'every night.' No exploration of co-occurring mental health concerns. Screening was superficial and incomplete.
dimension_scores.coaching_note: After establishing drinking pattern, ask about last drink, withdrawal history, and any mental health concerns to properly assess needs and urgency.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: Agent asked 'are you looking to come in soon or still deciding?' but did not probe for urgency signals. With daily drinking for years and failed attempts to cut back, agent should have asked about withdrawal risk and last use. No assessment of current safety or urgency level conducted.
dimension_scores.coaching_note: When someone reports daily drinking for years, always ask about last drink and withdrawal symptoms to assess medical urgency and appropriate level of care.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: Not applicable. Caller is the patient himself (David, calling about his own alcohol use).

Caller raised two concerns: nervousness about treatment with kids at home, and cost. Agent provided a brief solution for scheduling ('outpatient programs allow people to be home in the evenings') but did not acknowledge the emotional weight of the nervousness. Cost concern was dismissed with 'Your insurance should cover most of it' without exploring what 'most' means or offering concrete alternatives.
dimension_scores.coaching_note: Acknowledge the concern first ('I hear you—balancing treatment and family is a real worry for parents') before offering solutions, and be specific about cost coverage and alternatives.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: Agent provided vague next steps ('someone will call you to set something up') with imprecise timing ('within the next day or two'). No specific appointment set, no name of who would call, and caller had to prompt for timing and what to have ready. Ownership and specificity were both lacking.
dimension_scores.coaching_note: Set a specific callback time window, identify who will call, and proactively tell the caller what to have ready rather than waiting for them to ask.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: Agent avoided diagnostic language and did not make treatment outcome guarantees. Language remained within appropriate intake boundaries. One minor note: 'Your insurance should cover most of it' could be interpreted as a coverage guarantee without verification complete.
dimension_scores.coaching_note: Frame insurance coverage as 'we'll verify what your plan covers' rather than stating what it 'should' cover before verification is complete.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Okay, sure. How much are you drinking?

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Hi, I'm David. I'm looking into treatment for alcohol. My doctor suggested I call.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Every night. Okay. Do you have insurance?

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Yes, Summit Select.

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Yes, it's BP73195529.

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: I don't have that with me right now.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Okay, sure. How much are you drinking?

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Got it. Any prior treatment programs?

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: I mean, it's been going on for a few years. I tried to cut back on my own but it didn't really work.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Okay. And are you looking to come in soon or still deciding?

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: A lot. Pretty much every night. A six-pack or more.

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: I mean, it's been going on for a few years. I tried to cut back on my own but it didn't really work.

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Hi, I'm David. I'm looking into treatment for alcohol. My doctor suggested I call.

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: I think I want to do it but I'm nervous about it. I have kids, so I'm wondering how that works.

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: That's good to know. I'm worried about the cost too.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Your insurance should cover most of it. We'll verify.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: We'll have someone call you to set something up.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Within the next day or two.

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: When?

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Your insurance should cover most of it. We'll verify.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: We can work around schedules. Our outpatient programs allow people to be home in the evenings.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Okay, sure. How much are you drinking?
